questions.question: How many smaller squares remain after Step 1 in the Sierpinski Carpet?
questions.explanation: The center square is removed leaving 8 squares.

questions.question: Which property is essential for fractal geometry?
questions.explanation: Fractals repeat similar patterns at different scales.

questions.question: How many sides are present in the Koch Snowflake after Step 1?
questions.explanation: Each of the 3 sides becomes 4 segments giving 12 sides.

questions.question: Which view shows the height and width of an object?
questions.explanation: Front view displays height and width.

questions.question: Which solid has rectangular faces and two identical polygon bases?
questions.explanation: A prism has two identical bases connected by rectangular faces.

questions.question: Which view shows the object as seen from above?
questions.explanation: Top view represents the object as seen from above.

questions.question: Which of the following shapes can be formed from a cube net?
questions.explanation: Folding a cube net forms a cube.

questions.question: Which concept helps convert a 3D shape into a flat pattern?
questions.explanation: A net is a flattened representation of a solid.

questions.question: How many faces meet at a vertex of a cube?
questions.explanation: Three faces meet at each vertex of a cube.

questions.question: What is the main purpose of isometric drawing?
questions.explanation: Isometric drawings represent three dimensional objects on paper.

questions.question: Which of the following solids has triangular faces meeting at a single vertex?
questions.explanation: In a pyramid triangular faces meet at one vertex.

questions.question: What type of lines are used in isometric grids?
questions.explanation: Isometric grids use three sets of parallel lines.

questions.question: Which concept explains the shadow of an object on a surface?
questions.explanation: Projection represents the shadow or outline of an object.

questions.question: Which property remains unchanged in projection?
questions.explanation: Parallel lines remain parallel after projection.

questions.question: Which mathematical structure repeats infinitely in fractals?
questions.explanation: Fractals repeat patterns infinitely.

questions.question: Which figure has exactly 12 edges?
questions.explanation: A cube has 12 edges.

questions.question: What type of faces does a triangular prism have?
questions.explanation: A triangular prism has triangular bases and rectangular faces.

questions.question: Which method helps determine shortest paths on a cube surface?
questions.explanation: The cube is unfolded into a net to find shortest paths.

questions.question: Which view displays depth and height of an object?
questions.explanation: Side view shows height and depth.

questions.question: Which geometric topic combines mathematics and art patterns?
questions.explanation: Fractals produce artistic patterns based on mathematical rules.

questions.question: Which drawing technique uses equal angles between axes?
questions.explanation: Isometric drawing uses equal angles between axes.

questions.question: Which structure has five faces including the base?
questions.explanation: A square pyramid has four triangular faces and one base.

questions.question: Which figure forms repeating triangular holes in fractals?
questions.explanation: The Sierpinski Triangle contains repeating triangular holes.

questions.question: Which object helps visualize projections easily?
questions.explanation: Stacked cubes help understand projections.

questions.question: Which property of fractals allows patterns to repeat infinitely?
questions.explanation: Self similarity allows patterns to repeat infinitely.
